title: What can I do with Assistant?
summary: Use natural language when you know the outcome but not the exact screen or API.

items.title: Ask for an operation
items.text: Request validation, conversion, evidence search, workflow status, or pending-review help in plain language.

items.title: Attach context
items.text: Paste CSV, JSON, screenshots, or files when the Assistant needs source data. Sensitive changes remain review-gated.

items.title: Read the timeline
items.text: Planner text, tool calls, warnings, and final answers are shown in sequence; failures include request IDs for support.

nextAction: Start with: Validate this lab CSV and explain the issues with trusted evidence.

title: What can I do in Workbench?
summary: Create governed healthcare data workflows from inline data or uploaded files.

items.title: Choose the task
items.text: Describe the target outcome, such as validate, convert, profile FHIR-like data, or explain anomalies.

items.title: Provide source data
items.text: Paste structured data or upload a supported file. The backend records artifact hashes and source metadata.

items.title: Review before action
items.text: Meaning-changing transformations and low-confidence results pause for human approval before completion.

nextAction: Start with a small CSV or JSON sample before uploading a large document.

title: What can I do with Workflows?
summary: Inspect run status, validation issues, evidence, output artifacts, and audit events.

items.title: Find the run
items.text: Search and filter by workflow status, review state, and update time.

items.title: Inspect progress
items.text: Use steps for UI progress and events for audit traceability.

items.title: Open evidence and output
items.text: Check validation issues, retrieval evidence, limitations, and generated output before downstream use.

nextAction: Open the latest review-gated workflow first if you need an operational decision.

title: What can I do with Reviews?
summary: Approve, edit, reject, or investigate workflow decisions that require human control.

items.title: Read why it paused
items.text: Check the review reason, issue count, confidence signals, and evidence before deciding.

items.title: Approve only clear cases
items.text: Approve when the source data, validation findings, and intended transformation are consistent.

items.title: Reject unsafe output
items.text: Reject or edit when PHI, malformed data, missing units, or ambiguous clinical meaning is unresolved.

nextAction: Sort by newest pending reviews and resolve high-risk warnings first.

title: What can I do with Retrieval?
summary: Search trusted healthcare standards and inspect why evidence was ranked.

items.title: Search standards
items.text: Ask for schemas, FHIR-like resources, LOINC/UCUM guidance, validation rules, or medical evidence context.

items.title: Check grounding
items.text: Use score breakdowns, source diversity, query diagnostics, and evidence support before trusting a hit.

items.title: Improve weak results
items.text: Apply suggested filters, broaden narrow queries, or reindex trusted sources after corpus changes.

nextAction: Start with a concrete standard question, such as: What fields are required for lab_result_v1?

title: What can I do with Audit?
summary: Trace what happened to a workflow and when each backend action occurred.

items.title: Search by workflow
items.text: Select a run to inspect ordered events, status transitions, review gates, and output references.

items.title: Use request IDs
items.text: Match frontend errors and assistant stream replay events to backend diagnostics.

items.title: Verify sequence
items.text: Use audit events for traceability; use workflow steps for user-facing progress.

nextAction: Use the request ID from an error panel when investigating a failed operation.

title: What can I do with Schemas?
summary: Review supported data profiles, required fields, validation rules, and example formats.

items.title: Choose a profile
items.text: Use schema IDs such as lab_result_v1 when validating or converting clinical data.

items.title: Check required fields
items.text: Required fields drive validation issues and review gates.

items.title: Align input data
items.text: Use examples to rename columns or provide missing dates, units, and patient identifiers.

nextAction: Open lab_result_v1 before validating laboratory CSV data.

title: What can I do with Files?
summary: Inspect uploaded PDFs, images, spreadsheets, clipboard captures, and assistant attachments in the workspace.

items.title: Find source artifacts
items.text: Search by filename and filter by upload source to confirm what users attached or uploaded.

items.title: Check storage and hashes
items.text: Review MIME type, byte size, SHA-256, workspace ownership, and whether the object is in MinIO or legacy local storage.

items.title: Inspect extraction history
items.text: Open a file to see OCR/parse traces and access events before using the artifact in a workflow decision.

nextAction: Open the newest uploaded artifact after a Workbench or Assistant file parse.

title: What can I do in Settings?
summary: Inspect runtime readiness, migrations, storage, model, retrieval, and deployment controls.

items.title: Check readiness
items.text: Confirm Postgres, Redis, OAuth, LLM, embeddings, retrieval, jobs, and storage consistency before demos.

items.title: Adjust runtime controls
items.text: Change retrieval or assistant settings through validated backend settings APIs, not code edits.

items.title: Read migration state
items.text: Use migration diagnostics to catch duplicate versions, checksum drift, and pending database changes.

nextAction: Start by checking runtime readiness after any Docker, database, or environment change.

title: What can I do in Help?
summary: Learn the product vocabulary, workflows, and operator decision patterns.

items.title: Pick your role
items.text: Use role-based guides to understand whether you should upload data, review outputs, or inspect evidence.

items.title: Read examples
items.text: Use concrete CSV, validation, review, and retrieval examples before handling production-like data.

items.title: Escalate with diagnostics
items.text: Copy request IDs, workflow IDs, and error payloads when asking for support.

nextAction: Start with the role guide that matches your current task.
